description: ralph-end with open issues writes only .ralph-done
steps: Create a temp dir with a prd.json containing one issue with status 'open'
Run ralph-end <tempdir>
Verify <tempdir>/.ralph-done exists
Verify <tempdir>/.ralph-prd-done does NOT exist

description: ralph-end with all issues complete writes both sentinels
steps: Create a temp dir with a prd.json where all issues have status 'complete'
Run ralph-end <tempdir>
Verify <tempdir>/.ralph-done exists
Verify <tempdir>/.ralph-prd-done exists

description: ralph-end with no prd.json writes only .ralph-done without error
steps: Create a temp dir with no prd.json
Run ralph-end <tempdir>
Verify exit code is 0
Verify <tempdir>/.ralph-done exists
Verify <tempdir>/.ralph-prd-done does NOT exist

description: ralph-end with malformed prd.json writes only .ralph-done without error
steps: Create a temp dir with a prd.json containing invalid JSON
Run ralph-end <tempdir>
Verify exit code is 0
Verify <tempdir>/.ralph-done exists
Verify <tempdir>/.ralph-prd-done does NOT exist

description: ralph --max 3 runs exactly 3 iterations and creates 3 commits
steps: Create a temp git repo with a prd.json containing 5 open issues
Stub claude as a function that calls ralph-end <dir> and exits 0, making a file change each time
Stub git-commit-message to output 'test commit'
Run ralph --max 3 <dir>
Verify git log shows exactly 3 commits

description: ralph --max 10 exits early when prd.json has all issues complete
steps: Create a temp git repo with a prd.json where all issues have status 'complete'
Stub claude as a function that calls ralph-end <dir>, makes a file change, and exits 0
Stub git-commit-message to output 'test commit'
Run ralph --max 10 <dir>
Verify git log shows exactly 1 commit (early exit after first iteration)

description: ralph --max loop stops cleanly on Ctrl+C with no commit
steps: Create a temp git repo with a prd.json containing open issues
Stub claude as a function that makes a file change and exits 130 (SIGINT)
Run ralph --max 5 <dir>
Verify git log shows no commits
Verify no background processes remain from the loop

description: inactivity monitor plays sound once when inotifywait times out
steps: Stub inotifywait to exit immediately with code 2 (timeout)
Stub audio-play-oroshi to append its argument to a capture file
Stub claude to call ralph-end <dir> and exit 0 after a short delay
Run ralph --max 1 <dir>
Verify the capture file contains exactly one entry: 'ralph-timeout.mp3'

description: inactivity monitor resets after activity and can fire again
steps: Stub inotifywait to alternate: first call exits 0 (activity), second call exits 2 (timeout)
Stub audio-play-oroshi to append its argument to a capture file
Stub claude to call ralph-end <dir> and exit 0 after monitor has cycled twice
Run ralph --max 1 <dir>
Verify the capture file contains exactly one entry (alert fired after the second cycle, not the first)
